objectives: Introduce and practice vocabulary related to hobbies and leisure activities.
Develop listening and reading comprehension skills through dialogues.
Practice using present continuous tense and question forms.
Encourage students to express preferences and make invitations.

stages.name: Warm-up
stages.aim: To engage students and introduce the topic of leisure activities.
stages.content: Discussion about students' hobbies and leisure activities.

activities.action: Greet students and ask them about their hobbies. 'What do you like to do in your free time?' Write some of their answers on the board.
Respond to the teacher's questions about their hobbies.

stages.name: Stage 1: Listen and Read
stages.aim: To practice listening and reading comprehension.
stages.target_language: DIY, Knitting kit, Leisure time, Hang out, Cinema, Comedy
stages.content: Listening to and reading the conversation between Tom and Trang.

activities.action: Play the audio of the conversation. Ask students to listen and follow along in their textbooks.
Listen to the audio and read along in their textbooks.

stages.name: Stage 2: Complete the Sentences
stages.aim: To check comprehension of the dialogue.
stages.target_language: Knitting kit, Leisure time, Friends, Cinema, Comedy
stages.content: Complete sentences based on the conversation.

activities.action: Instruct students to read the conversation again and complete the sentences in their textbooks.
Read the conversation and fill in the blanks in the sentences provided.

stages.name: Stage 3: Activity Matching
stages.aim: To associate vocabulary with images.
stages.target_language: DIY, Puzzles, Surfing the net, Cooking, Playing sport, Messaging friends
stages.content: Matching activities with images.

activities.action: [IMAGE_TAG_26]
Show the images and ask students to match the activities from the box to the correct pictures.
Work in pairs to discuss and match the activities with the pictures.

stages.name: Stage 4: Guess the Activities
stages.aim: To infer activities from descriptive phrases.
stages.target_language: Improve memory, Save money, Improve physical health, Keep in touch, Learn IT
stages.content: Guessing activities based on descriptions.

activities.action: Read the descriptive phrases and ask students to guess which activities they describe.
Work in pairs to discuss and guess the activities described by the phrases.

stages.name: Stage 5: Group Discussion
stages.aim: To practice speaking and expressing future plans.
stages.content: Discussing weekend plans and reporting to the class.

activities.action: Ask students to discuss in groups what they will do if they have free time this weekend.
Discuss in groups and prepare to report their answers to the class.

stages.name: Wrap-up
stages.aim: To review and consolidate the lesson's content.
stages.target_language: Review of key vocabulary and phrases
stages.content: Summarizing the lesson and addressing any questions.

activities.action: Review the key vocabulary and phrases learned in the lesson. Answer any remaining questions from students.
Ask any questions they have and listen to the summary.

objectives: Understand and use expressions about likes and dislikes
Practice pronunciation of /ʊ/ and /u:/ sounds
Complete sentences using appropriate vocabulary

stages.name: Warm-up
stages.aim: To introduce the topic of hobbies and likes/dislikes
stages.content: Engage students in a brief discussion about their hobbies and what they like or dislike doing.

activities.action: Greet the students and ask them to share one hobby they enjoy.
Respond to the teacher's question with their favorite hobby.

stages.name: Vocabulary Introduction
stages.aim: To introduce vocabulary related to likes and dislikes
stages.target_language: be fond of, be keen on, be interested in, be crazy about, be into
stages.content: Introduce expressions about likes and dislikes using the textbook vocabulary.

activities.action: Introduce the vocabulary words: be fond of, be keen on, be interested in, be crazy about, be into.
Explain how to express dislikes by adding 'not' after the verb 'be'.
Provide examples: 'She is keen on cooking.' → 'She is not keen on cooking.'
Listen and take notes on the new vocabulary and grammar explanation.

stages.name: Task 1: Matching Exercise
stages.aim: To practice matching expressions about likes
stages.target_language: be fond of, be keen on, be interested in, be crazy about, be into
stages.content: Match a word/phrase in column A with a word in column B to make expressions about likes.

activities.action: [IMAGE_TAG_39]
Display the matching exercise on the board.
Work in pairs to match the words in column A with the correct words in column B.
Check answers as a class and provide feedback.

stages.name: Task 2: Fill in the Blanks
stages.aim: To practice using vocabulary in context
stages.content: Fill in each blank with a correct word from the box.

activities.action: [IMAGE_TAG_42]
Explain the task and read the sentences aloud.
Individually fill in the blanks with the appropriate words.
Review the answers with the class and correct any mistakes.

stages.name: Task 3: Sentence Completion
stages.aim: To personalize language use by completing sentences about personal likes/dislikes
stages.target_language: cooking, doing DIY, surfing the net, playing sport, doing puzzles, messaging friends
stages.content: Complete the sentences about what you like or dislike doing.

activities.action: [IMAGE_TAG_46]
Explain the task and provide examples.
Complete the sentences with words from the box or their own ideas.
Ask some students to share their sentences with the class.

stages.name: Pronunciation Practice
stages.aim: To practice pronunciation of /ʊ/ and /u:/ sounds
stages.target_language: cook, school, group, would, push, woman, June, move
stages.content: Listen and repeat the words. Pay attention to the sounds /ʊ/ and /u:/ and categorize them.

activities.action: [IMAGE_TAG_50]
Play the audio for pronunciation practice.
Listen and repeat the words after the audio.
Write the words in the correct column (/ʊ/ or /u:/) in their notebooks.

stages.name: Sentence Practice
stages.aim: To practice pronunciation in context
stages.content: Listen and practice the sentences. Underline /ʊ/ and circle /u:/ words.

activities.action: Play the audio of the sentences.
Listen to the sentences and practice repeating them.
Underline the words with /ʊ/ and circle the words with /u:/ in their textbooks.
Review the sentences and ensure students have identified the sounds correctly.

stages.name: Wrap-up
stages.aim: To review the lesson and reinforce learning
stages.target_language: be fond of, be keen on, be interested in, be crazy about, be into
stages.content: Review key vocabulary and pronunciation from the lesson.

activities.action: Ask students to share one new thing they learned today.
Share their learning with the class.
Summarize the lesson and assign any homework if necessary.

objectives: Understand and use verbs of liking/disliking followed by gerunds and to-infinitives.
Correctly categorize verbs based on their grammatical usage.
Complete sentences using the correct form of verbs of liking/disliking.
Express personal preferences using verbs of liking/disliking.
Engage in a group activity to practice using verbs of liking/disliking in questions.

stages.name: Warm-up
stages.aim: To introduce the topic of liking and disliking.
stages.content: Introduction to verbs of liking and disliking.

activities.action: Greet the students and introduce the topic of the lesson: verbs of liking and disliking.
Ask students to share activities they like or dislike doing in their free time.

stages.name: Stage 1: Grammar Introduction
stages.aim: To introduce the grammar rules for verbs of liking/disliking + gerunds and to-infinitives.
stages.target_language: Verbs of liking/disliking + gerunds; Verbs of liking/disliking + to-infinitives
stages.content: Explanation of how some verbs of liking and disliking are followed by gerunds or to-infinitives.

activities.action: Explain that some verbs of liking/disliking are followed by gerunds only, while others can be followed by both gerunds and to-infinitives.
[IMAGE_TAG_57]
Provide examples: 'They dislike surfing the net.' and 'I love going/to go to the cinema.'

stages.name: Stage 2: Categorizing Verbs
stages.aim: To categorize verbs into those followed by gerunds only and those followed by both gerunds and to-infinitives.
stages.target_language: love, dislike, like, enjoy, detest, hate, fancy, prefer
stages.content: Categorization of verbs based on their grammatical usage.

activities.action: Display the image with the list of verbs and columns for categorization.
[IMAGE_TAG_58]
Work in pairs to categorize the verbs into the appropriate columns.
Review the answers with the class.

stages.name: Stage 3: Multiple Choice Exercise
stages.aim: To practice using the correct form of verbs in sentences.
stages.target_language: travelling, doing, to watch, going, hunting
stages.content: Multiple choice questions to choose the correct verb form.

activities.action: Instruct students to choose the correct answer (A, B, or C) for each sentence.
Complete the exercise individually.
Review the correct answers with the class.

stages.name: Stage 4: Sentence Completion with Pictures
stages.aim: To complete sentences using the correct form of verbs of liking/disliking.
stages.content: Complete sentences using verbs in brackets.

activities.action: [IMAGE_TAG_65]
Display the image and instruct students to complete the sentences using the verbs in brackets.
Work individually to complete the sentences.
Check the answers as a class.

stages.name: Stage 5: Personal Sentences
stages.aim: To express personal preferences using verbs of liking/disliking.
stages.target_language: like, prefer, love, dislike, hate, detest
stages.content: Students complete sentences about themselves.

activities.action: [IMAGE_TAG_68]
Ask students to complete the sentences about themselves using the verbs provided.
Write their sentences individually.
Invite a few students to share their sentences with the class.

stages.name: Stage 6: Game - Likes and Dislikes Mimes
stages.aim: To practice using verbs of liking/disliking in questions through a mime game.
stages.content: Group activity where students mime activities and guess using questions.

activities.action: Explain the rules of the mime game: one student mimes an activity they like or dislike, others guess by asking Yes/No questions.
Participate in the game, taking turns to mime and guess.
Provide feedback and correct any mistakes in the use of verbs.

stages.name: Wrap-up
stages.aim: To review and consolidate the lesson's content.
stages.content: Summary of the lesson and key points.

activities.action: Summarize the main points of the lesson: verbs of liking/disliking and their grammatical forms.
Ask students if they have any questions about the lesson.

objectives: Understand and use language for inviting and accepting invitations.
Identify and discuss various leisure activities from around the world.
Practice speaking skills through pair and group activities.

stages.name: Warm-up
stages.aim: To introduce the topic of leisure activities and invitations.
stages.content: Introduction to the lesson topic: leisure activities and how to invite someone.

activities.action: Greet the class and introduce the topic of leisure activities and invitations.
Ask students about their favorite leisure activities.
Respond to the teacher's questions about their favorite leisure activities.

stages.name: Stage 1: Listen and Read
stages.aim: To familiarize students with inviting and accepting invitations through dialogues.
stages.target_language: Would you like to...?; Do you fancy...?; I’d love to.; That’s great.
stages.content: Students listen to and read dialogues focusing on inviting and accepting invitations.

activities.action: Play the audio recording of the dialogues.
Listen to the dialogues and follow along in their textbooks.
[IMAGE_TAG_75]
Highlight the expressions used for inviting and accepting invitations on the board.

stages.name: Stage 2: Pair Practice
stages.aim: To practice inviting and accepting invitations using given situations.
stages.target_language: Would you like to...?; Do you fancy...?; I’d love to.; That’s great.
stages.content: Students practice inviting and accepting invitations in pairs using specific scenarios.

activities.action: Explain the task and provide example dialogues.
Work in pairs to practice inviting and accepting invitations in the provided situations: playing badminton, making paper flowers, trying home-made pizza.
Monitor pairs and provide feedback.

stages.name: Stage 3: Picture Activity
stages.aim: To identify leisure activities from pictures.
stages.content: Students work in pairs to identify activities in pictures.

activities.action: Show the image with pictures of leisure activities.
[IMAGE_TAG_80]
Discuss in pairs what the activity in each picture is.
Elicit answers from the class and write them on the board.

stages.name: Stage 4: Reading and Completing Table
stages.aim: To read about teenagers' leisure activities and complete a table with information.
stages.target_language: Origami, Snowboarding, Badminton, Relax, Improve, Muscle strength, Reduce stress
stages.content: Students read about leisure activities and complete a table with details.

activities.action: Ask students to read the text about Sakura, Eric, and Lan's leisure activities.
[IMAGE_TAG_83]
Complete the table with information from the text.
[IMAGE_TAG_84]
Check answers as a class.

stages.name: Stage 5: Group Discussion
stages.aim: To discuss and express opinions on leisure activities.
stages.content: Students discuss which leisure activity they would like to try and why.

activities.action: Form groups and ask students to discuss which activity they want to try and why.
Discuss in groups and prepare to report their answers.
Ask each group to report their answers to the class.

stages.name: Wrap-up
stages.aim: To review the lesson and conclude the class.
stages.target_language: Review of key expressions and vocabulary
stages.content: Review the key points of the lesson and conclude the class.

activities.action: Summarize the key expressions and vocabulary learned in the lesson.
Ask any remaining questions.
Provide homework related to inviting and leisure activities.

objectives: Identify and discuss various leisure activities.
Improve reading comprehension through answering questions about a text.
Practice speaking skills by discussing personal leisure activities.
materials: Printed copies of the reading text

stages.name: Warm-up
stages.aim: To engage students and introduce the topic of leisure activities.
stages.content: Discuss common leisure activities.

activities.action: Greet the students and ask them about their weekend activities.
Introduce the topic of leisure activities.
Share their weekend activities briefly.

stages.name: Stage 1: Picture Discussion
stages.aim: To activate prior knowledge and vocabulary related to leisure activities.
stages.content: Look at the pictures. What activities can you see?

activities.action: Display pictures of various leisure activities.
Discuss in pairs what activities they see in the pictures.
Elicit responses from students and write key vocabulary on the board.

stages.name: Stage 2: Reading Comprehension
stages.aim: To develop reading skills and understand the text about Trang's leisure activities.
stages.content: Read about Trang's leisure activities. Choose the correct answer.

activities.action: Distribute copies of the reading text about Trang's leisure activities.
Read the text silently.
Ask students to choose the correct answer for the questions provided in the textbook.
Select answers: A. in the past, B. with her friends, C. with her family.

stages.name: Stage 3: Vocabulary Meaning
stages.aim: To understand the meaning of vocabulary in context.
stages.content: The word 'connect' is closest in meaning to ______.

activities.action: Discuss the word 'connect' and its meanings.
Choose the correct meaning: A. join with something, B. better understand someone, C. speak to someone by phone.

stages.name: Stage 4: Detailed Reading
stages.aim: To practice detailed reading and answer comprehension questions.
stages.content: Read the text again and answer the questions.

activities.action: Ask students to read the text again.
Answer the detailed questions about the text.
Review answers with the class.

stages.name: Stage 5: Group Discussion
stages.aim: To practice speaking skills and discuss personal experiences.
stages.content: Work in groups. Take turns to ask and answer the questions.

activities.action: Organize students into small groups.
Discuss the given questions in their groups.
[IMAGE_TAG_110]
Record their friends' answers in the table.

stages.name: Stage 6: Reporting
stages.aim: To practice public speaking and summarize group discussions.
stages.content: Report your group members' answers to the class. What activities are the most common?

activities.action: Ask each group to report their findings.
Present their group's answers to the class.
Facilitate a class discussion on common activities.

stages.name: Wrap-up
stages.aim: To consolidate learning and wrap up the lesson.
stages.content: Summarize the lesson's key points.

activities.action: Summarize the lesson and highlight key vocabulary and grammar points.
Reflect on what they have learned.

objectives: To develop students' listening skills through comprehension questions.
To enhance students' speaking skills by discussing leisure activities.
To improve students' writing skills by composing an email about free time activities.
materials: Audio recording of an interview with Mark

stages.name: Warm-up
stages.aim: To introduce the topic of leisure activities and activate prior knowledge.
stages.target_language: Leisure time, activities, free time, friends
stages.content: Discussing leisure activities with friends.

activities.action: Greet the students and ask them about their weekend activities.
Share their weekend activities with the class.

stages.name: Stage 1: Answer the Questions
stages.aim: To encourage students to think about leisure activities and their importance.
stages.content: 1. In your opinion, what activities can we do with our friends in our leisure time? 2. Why should we spend time with our friends?

activities.action: Write the questions on the board and ask students to discuss in pairs.
Discuss the questions in pairs and share their thoughts with the class.

stages.name: Stage 2: Listening Comprehension
stages.aim: To practice listening for specific information.
stages.content: Listen to an interview with Mark about his leisure activities. Choose the correct answer.

activities.action: Play the audio recording of Mark's interview.
Listen to the interview and choose the correct answers from the options given.
Check the answers with the class.

stages.name: Stage 3: Listening for Details
stages.aim: To enhance listening skills by filling in missing information.
stages.content: Listen to the interview again. Fill in each blank in the table with no more than TWO words.

activities.action: Play the audio recording again and provide students with the worksheet.
Fill in the blanks in the table while listening.
[IMAGE_TAG_128]
Review the answers with the class.

stages.name: Stage 4: Speaking Practice
stages.aim: To develop speaking skills through pair work.
stages.content: Work in pairs. Ask and answer the questions: 1. When do you usually have free time? 2. What do you usually do with your friends in your free time? 3. Why do you do these activities?

activities.action: Instruct students to work in pairs and discuss the questions.
Engage in a dialogue with their partner, asking and answering the questions.

stages.name: Stage 5: Writing an Email
stages.aim: To practice writing skills by composing an email about leisure activities.
stages.content: Write an email (80 - 100 words) to a penfriend to tell him/her about what you usually do with your friends in your free time. Use your answers in task 4.

activities.action: Explain the email structure and provide the starting and ending format.
[IMAGE_TAG_135]
Write their email individually, using the provided format.
Collect and review the emails, providing feedback.

stages.name: Wrap-up
stages.aim: To review the lesson's content and consolidate learning.
stages.content: Summarize the lesson and discuss what students learned.

activities.action: Ask students to share one new thing they learned today.
Reflect on the lesson and share their thoughts with the class.

objectives: Review and practice vocabulary related to leisure activities.
Practice forming sentences using gerunds to express likes and dislikes.
Improve sentence construction skills using given cues.
Enhance understanding of verb forms in different contexts.

stages.name: Warm-up
stages.aim: To engage students and introduce the topic of leisure activities.
stages.content: Teacher will introduce the topic by asking students about their favorite leisure activities.

activities.action: Greet students and ask, 'What do you like to do in your free time?'.
Respond with their favorite leisure activities.
Write students' responses on the board and introduce the lesson's focus on leisure activities.

stages.name: Stage 1: Complete the sentences with appropriate leisure activities
stages.aim: To practice using vocabulary related to leisure activities in context.
stages.target_language: Online, Puzzles, Crafts, Messaging, Sports, Internet
stages.content: Students complete sentences using appropriate leisure activities.

activities.action: Explain the task and read each sentence aloud.
Fill in the blanks with appropriate leisure activities.
Check answers as a class and provide feedback.

stages.name: Stage 2: Write complete sentences from the given cues
stages.aim: To practice sentence construction using given cues.
stages.target_language: Crazy about, Interested in, Not fond of, Not into, Keen on
stages.content: Students write complete sentences using the given cues.

activities.action: Explain the task and provide an example using the first cue.
Work individually to write sentences from the cues.
Ask students to share their sentences and provide feedback.

stages.name: Stage 3: Fill in each blank with the correct form(s) of the verb in brackets
stages.aim: To practice using gerunds in sentences.
stages.content: Students fill in blanks with the correct form of verbs in brackets.

activities.action: Explain the task and read each sentence aloud.
Complete sentences with the correct verb forms.
Review answers with the class and correct any mistakes.

stages.name: Stage 4: Complete the passage
stages.aim: To use verb forms in context and improve comprehension.
stages.target_language: Ride, Read, Message, Make, Knit, Play
stages.content: Students complete a passage using verbs in brackets and an image.

activities.action: Display the passage and image [IMAGE_TAG_159] on the screen.
Complete the passage using the correct verb forms.
Discuss the completed passage with the class and provide feedback.

stages.name: Wrap-up
stages.aim: To review the lesson and reinforce learning.
stages.content: Review of key vocabulary and grammar points from the lesson.

activities.action: Ask students to recall the leisure activities discussed.
Share what they learned about forming sentences with gerunds.
Summarize the lesson and assign any homework if necessary.

objectives: Conduct interviews to gather information about leisure activities.
Practice using verbs of liking/disliking followed by gerunds and/or to-infinitives.
Report findings using structured questions.
Enhance pronunciation of sounds /uː/ and /ʌ/.

stages.name: Warm-up
stages.aim: To introduce the topic of leisure activities and engage students.
stages.target_language: Leisure activities, free time, likes and dislikes.
stages.content: Discussion about what students like to do in their free time.

activities.action: Greet the class and ask students about their favorite leisure activities.
Share their favorite leisure activities with the class.

stages.name: Stage 1: Conduct Interviews
stages.aim: To gather information about students' leisure activities.
stages.target_language: When do you have free time? What do you like doing with your family/friends?
stages.content: Students interview each other using provided questions.

activities.action: Explain the task of interviewing classmates and provide the list of questions.

Work in groups to interview classmates using the questions: 1. When do you have free time? 2. What do you like doing with your family in your free time? 3. What do you like doing with your friends in your free time? 4. What leisure activities do you like doing on your own? 5. What leisure activities would you like to try in the future?

stages.name: Stage 2: Collect Answers
stages.aim: To organize the collected data from interviews.
stages.target_language: Student, Question 1, Question 2, Question 3, Question 4, Question 5
stages.content: Students fill in a table with the interview answers.

activities.action: Show [IMAGE_TAG_172] and explain how to fill in the table with the collected data.
[IMAGE_TAG_172]
Fill in the table with answers from their interviews.

stages.name: Stage 3: Report Findings
stages.aim: To practice reporting skills and use of structured questions.
stages.target_language: How many students did you interview? When do the students have free time?
stages.content: Students report their findings to the class using guiding questions.

activities.action: Provide the guiding questions for reporting: 1. How many students did you interview? 2. When do the students have free time? 3. What are the most popular activities they do with their family? 4. What are the most popular activities they do with their friends? 5. What are the most popular activities they do on their own? 6. What leisure activities would they like to try in the future?
Present their findings to the class, using the guiding questions to structure their report.

stages.name: Wrap-up
stages.aim: To review the lesson and reinforce key concepts.
stages.content: Review of the lesson and pronunciation practice.

activities.action: Summarize the lesson and ask students to pronounce words with /uː/ and /ʌ/.
Practice pronunciation of words with /uː/ and /ʌ/ sounds.
[IMAGE_TAG_180]
